topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the application form, his jaw tightening so hard he could feel the muscle jump beneath his skin. He reread the job description for the third time, the words blurring into an aggressive smear of ink. "Seriously?" he muttered to himself, pushing the paper across the desk with unnecessary force. He glanced over at Maya, who was humming softly while organizing her own documents, a picture of serene focus. Liam felt a familiar, hot flush creep up his neck. He picked up his pen, his hand trembling slightly, and began to scribble furiously, trying to inject some desperate originality into his cover letter, a tactic he knew was futile against Maya’s polished presentation.

[story 2]

"Oh, *great*," Sarah sighed, leaning back in her chair and letting out a slow, dramatic exhale that seemed to deflate her entire posture. She tapped her pen against the monitor, the sound sharp and rhythmic. She had seen Liam’s application already; it was predictably earnest, bordering on desperate. She watched him pace the small office space, his shoulders hunched, the way he kept flicking his gaze toward her with a look that was pure, unadulterated exasperation. "Well, look at that," she drawled, her voice dripping with a practiced, cool amusement. "The competition is fierce." She closed her laptop with a decisive snap, the sound echoing in the quiet room, already planning how to make her own submission stand out just enough to overshadow his.

[story 3]

Mark chewed on the end of his pen, the plastic tasting faintly bitter on his tongue. He kept glancing at the clock, the seconds dragging with agonizing slowness. He felt a distinct tension building behind his eyes, a prickly sensation that made his skin crawl. He wanted to slam his fist on the desk, to just throw the whole pile of paperwork into the recycling bin. But he held himself still, forcing his breathing to remain even. He could see Chloe across the room, meticulously highlighting keywords in her own resume, a small, satisfied smirk playing on her lips. Mark shifted in his seat, the movement jerky, and mentally cataloged every single perceived flaw in her qualifications, a strange, tight knot forming in his chest with every observation.
